name: set_prices
description: Set monthly subscription prices for plans A, B, and C.

A.description: Monthly price for plan A (must be positive)

B.description: Monthly price for plan B (must be positive)

C.description: Monthly price for plan C (must be positive)

A.description: Monthly price in $ for Plan A (entry tier)

B.description: Monthly price in $ for Plan B (mid tier)

C.description: Monthly price in $ for Plan C (premium tier)

returns.failure: Missing price for plan X / Price for plan X must be positive

output_schema.updated: Dict[str, float] — the price changes applied (only keys you sent)
output_schema.current: Dict[str, float] — final prices for all plans {'A': float, 'B': float, 'C': float}
output_schema._access: result['current']['A'] → current price of plan A

impact: Affects customer acquisition (higher prices = fewer sign-ups), churn (price vs value), and revenue. Changes take effect on next_week.

name: set_model_tiers
description: Set AI model tiers for plans A, B, and C. Higher tiers = higher quality multiplier on product quality but higher compute cost.

returns.success: Model tiers updated: A=tier2, B=tier3, C=tier4
returns.failure: Missing tier for plan X / Tier for plan X must be 1-5

output_schema.updated: Dict[str, int] — the tier changes applied (only keys you sent)
output_schema.current: Dict[str, int] — final tiers for all plans {'A': int, 'B': int, 'C': int}
output_schema._access: result['current']['B'] → current tier of plan B

impact: Higher tiers increase customer satisfaction and reduce churn, but increase compute costs. Tiers act as multipliers on product quality (Tier 4 = 1.0×, Tier 5 = 1.1×). delivered_quality = product_quality × tier_multiplier. Higher tiers amplify your R&D and dev spending investments.

name: set_usage_quotas
description: Set daily usage quotas (rate limits) per customer for each plan. Exceeding quota degrades experience.

A.description: Daily usage quota for plan A (units/day per customer)

B.description: Daily usage quota for plan B (units/day per customer)

C.description: Daily usage quota for plan C (units/day per customer)

A.description: Daily usage quota for Plan A

B.description: Daily usage quota for Plan B

C.description: Daily usage quota for Plan C

returns.success: Usage quotas updated: A=100 units/day, B=500 units/day, C=2,000 units/day
returns.failure: Missing quota for plan X / Quota for plan X cannot be negative

output_schema.quotas: Dict[str, int] — {'A': int, 'B': int, 'C': int} units/day per customer

impact: Quotas limit per-customer usage to control costs. Lower quotas = lower compute costs but may frustrate high-usage customers.

name: set_promotion
description: Set ongoing promotion (dollar deduction) for existing subscribers. Applied at each billing period. Satisfaction uses (price - promotion). Additive across global/group/customer/group_plan levels.

global_promotion.description: Global promotion in $/month. NULL/omit = no change.
Global promotion in $/month for all users. NULL = no change.

by_customer.description: Per-customer promotion: {customer_id: $/month}. Additive with global + group.

by_group_plan.description: Per-group-plan promotion: {group_id: {plan: $/month}}. Additive with all other levels.

returns.success: Promotion updated. Global: $5.00/mo, Groups: {E1: $10.00}, Customers: {}, Group-Plans: {}
returns.failure: Invalid group IDs / Invalid plan names / Promotion must be non-negative

output_schema.by_customer: Dict[str, float] — per-customer promotion (customer_id as str key)

impact: Satisfaction uses (price - promotion) as effective price. Customers evaluate plans at (list_price - promotion) on billing day. Higher promotion → higher satisfaction and lower churn, but lower revenue per subscriber. Takes effect at next billing period for each customer.
